commit.message:
Add GitHub Actions workflow for daily briefing pipeline

Move the briefing pipeline from macOS launchd to GitHub Actions to
eliminate Apple Silicon DarkWake failures on battery with lid closed.
The pipeline now runs on an Ubuntu cloud runner at 6am ET daily.

Changes:
- New .github/workflows/morning-briefing.yml with dual cron schedule
  (EST/EDT), dependency installation, and log artifact upload
- morning-briefing.sh: CI-aware environment detection (GITHUB_WORKSPACE),
  skip macOS-specific code (caffeinate, osascript, lockfile) in CI
- publish.sh: CI-aware BRIEFING_DIR path
- Also includes previously-applied fixes: exec in research_call,
  retry_if_empty for failed sub-steps

Local Mac pipeline continues to work as a fallback.

commit.message:
fix(security): resolve DNS once and reuse for SSRF validation to prevent rebinding (#518)

* fix(security): resolve DNS once and reuse for SSRF validation to prevent rebinding

The previous SSRF protection resolved DNS in validate_url() to check IPs
against a blocklist, but then reqwest independently re-resolved DNS when
making the actual HTTP connection.  Between validation and connection, a
DNS rebinding attack could flip the record from a public IP (passes
validation) to a private IP like 169.254.169.254 (AWS metadata endpoint).

Fix: split URL validation into two phases:
- validate_url(): synchronous URL structure checks (scheme, localhost,
  IP literals) -- no DNS resolution
- validate_and_resolve_url(): async DNS resolution via
  tokio::net::lookup_host, validates all resolved IPs, returns
  SocketAddrs
- build_pinned_client(): constructs a per-request reqwest Client with
  resolve() pinning so reqwest connects to the pre-validated IPs without
  a second DNS lookup

Applied to both HttpTool and WebFetchTool.  WebFetchTool builds a fresh
pinned client per redirect hop, ensuring DNS rebinding cannot occur at
any point in a redirect chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add V18: V15 arch (5 heads) + programmatic geo data + geo freq ramp

V17 no-bottleneck experiment confirmed that FR/ES translation heads are
essential for natural direction consistency (dir_con 0.35 in V15 vs 0.20
in V17). V18 restores V15 architecture with 5 heads (FR/ES/Para/Parse)
and adds: programmatic geometry data from GeometryDataGenerator with
train/test vocab splits, geo frequency ramp (every step for 2K then
ramp to 1/5 over 10K), and 6L decoders.

docs: Nanz Language Book v3 — complete English rewrite

1559 lines covering everything shipped plus new features:
- range(lo..hi) counter-based iteration — one instruction per loop body
- Dual-VM compile-time asserts (MIR2 VM + Z80 binary by default)
- via mir2 / via z80 assert syntax + rationale
- * 256 strength reduction (LD H,L / LD L,0 — 7× faster)
- Parallel copy A↔B cycle fix + how dual-VM caught it
- MIR2→C backend: why (cross-check, portability, reference semantics)
- MIR2→QBE backend: why (modern target, IR validation, E2E testing)
- u32/ClassDWord via EXX shadow pair
- Fixed-point types f8.8/f16.8/etc.
- ^Struct pointer receivers, HL-chain store, use-before-init warnings
- BranchEquiv, CondRetSink+CmpSubCarry, PBQP+coalescing
- Verified codegen showcase: abs_diff (4 insts), sum_range (1 inst/iter),
  pixel_addr (byte-swap), sin LUT (3 insts), add32 (5 insts)

Wire Value-Guided Search (VGS) into training and inference pipeline

Training:
- Accumulate rollout data across iterations for value model training
- Train lightweight ValueModel (linear + sigmoid, BCE loss) after OAPL
- Save value_model.json alongside LoRA adapter in checkpoints
- Rich status spinner for value model training step

Inference:
- Add use_vgs, vgs_candidate_width, vgs_max_depth params to solve()
- Auto-detect: use VGS when value model is loaded, else Parallel Thinking
- VGS adapter bridges BaseAgent.generate_step() to VGS's expand() interface
  (handles tool calls, search execution, terminal detection)
- Weighted majority vote aggregation using value model scores

Loading:
- Agent.load() restores value model from checkpoint
- _value_model attribute initialized in __init__

Tests: 8 new tests covering value model fit/save/load, VGS engine,
agent integration, and fallback to Parallel Thinking.

commit.message:
topology: VDD-ramp startup for current-starved ring osc

Key changes:
- VDD ramps from 0→1.8V in 1ns with uic to avoid slow DC operating point
- Ckick=50fF on n1 breaks symmetry during power-up
- Removed .ic/uic approach (circuit latched at DC)
- Removed varactors (killed frequency)
- 30ns simulation time with rise=3,4 for fast DE evaluation
- evaluate.py: Vctrl sweep at 0.6/0.9/1.8V, timeout=60s

commit.message:
fix(shipping): check accounts.from in charges_details + $299 rule on all paths

ML includes shipping charges in charges_details even when buyer pays.
Previously only the API recovery path had the <$299 check. Now all 3 paths
are consistent:
1. charges_details: check accounts.from !== 'buyer' before recording
2. payload: apply $299 threshold
3. API recovery: already had $299 threshold

Added diagnostic logging for shipping charges to capture ML's full charge
structure for verification.

commit.message:
fix(security): address prompt injection bypass and network exposure issues (#383)

* fix(security): address prompt injection bypass and network exposure issues

- sanitize: fix stripHiddenHTMLBlocks to continue scanning after a
  non-hidden tag rather than breaking, preventing hidden elements that
  follow visible elements from being missed (prompt injection bypass)
- dashboard: reject empty host in validateLoopback — an empty host binds
  to all interfaces (0.0.0.0), not loopback as the old comment claimed
- dashboard: validate itemID in control endpoints (stop/retry/message)
  the same way sessionID is validated in handleLogs
- dashboard: cap the ?tail= query parameter at 10,000 lines to bound
  memory usage from oversized requests
- claude: create stream log files with 0600 permissions so conversation
  content (tool calls, file contents) is not world-readable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #383

- validateLoopback: resolve "localhost" via LookupIP instead of
  special-casing it, so misconfigured DNS can't bypass loopback check
- Centralize ID validation: reuse validateItemID for sessionID in
  handleLogs to prevent the two validations from drifting
- Stream log chmod: explicitly chmod existing files to 0600 after
  opening so upgrades remediate pre-existing world-readable logs
- Fix maxTailLines comment: accurately says "cap log response size"
  instead of "limit memory usage" (ReadSessionLog reads the full file)
- Strengthen TestHandleLogs_TailCapped: writes a temp stream log with
  >maxTailLines entries and asserts response contains exactly
  maxTailLines lines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Render bidirectional subway edges as parallel coloured tracks

When traffic flows both ways between two stations (different arc colours),
draw two side-by-side lines offset perpendicularly — like real subway maps
where multiple lines share the same track. Single-direction edges remain
as one solid line. Removes the gradient defs which are no longer needed.

commit.message:
fix: Clover payment status bug — orders no longer auto-confirm on redirect

Two bugs caused orders to show as payment_received before the customer paid:

BUG 1 (Critical): order-confirmed/page.tsx auto-confirmed any Clover order
on redirect, even on cancellation/timeout. Page is now read-only — shows a
'Payment being verified' notice if webhook hasn't fired yet.

BUG 2 (Silent): webhook stored checkoutSessionId as payment_reference but
matched against cloverOrderId (different IDs). Now passes externalReferenceId
(our Supabase UUID) to Clover checkout; webhook matches by that field first.

Added: scripts/test-payment-flow.mjs (npm run test:payment)
All 10 smoke tests pass: pending to redirect to still-pending to webhook to confirmed
